raw_consumer_statement:
I have a credit card with Citi, its the XXXX XXXX XXXX card and I use it strictly for business travel. I noticed about XXXX weeks ago that I had several unauthorized charges to my account and I raised a fraud case with Citi. I have waited XXXX weeks for the charges to be taken care of ( I have been a victim of identity theft in the past and they were resolved quickly with my back ) but after XXXX weeks I received a letter in the mail dated XX/XX/24 stating Citi determined no fraud. I received the letter on XXXX and immediately called Citi for an explanation. I called at XXXX EST and spoke with XXXX who was not able to connect me to anyone to give me an explanation, I asked to have my account terminated and that I would be submitting a complaint with consumer finance and the Office of the Comptroller. XXXX then transferred me to an XXXX who was his supervisor. I did not catch the gentlemans name, but he explained that he was not anyones supervisor and that he worked in the forbearance department and could not help me. He transferred me to the Fraud department who then told me that they were having system issues and could not look up my account, then promptly hung up on me.

The reality is that I never authorized any of the transactions as this card is strictly for business travel. I would like assistance in have these fraudulent charges reversed so I do not have to unfairly bear the burden for {$1900.00} in unauthorized and unknown charges.
